items.description: The crew, who are accomplished pilots, engineers and scientists, reveal their hopes, fears and family sacrifices.

items.title: Gaza's evacuated premature babies return to devastated strip
items.description: Eleven children born prematurely, who were later evacuated to Egypt during the war, have returned to Gaza. The children were moved in the first weeks of the conflict when the hospital where they were being cared for was besieged by the Israeli military. The return to their families has been marked by uncertainty in a Gaza Strip devastated by two and a half years of war.

items.title: Train services in south-east Qld to be disrupted due to industrial action
items.description: Queensland commuters have been warned to expect disruptions to train services on Wednesday as workers take industrial action over a "bargaining dispute".

items.title: Teen cyclist killed at Road World Championships not found for 82 minutes
items.description: A cyclist who was killed during the Road World Championships was not found for 82 minutes after a crash, investigators find.

items.title: One dead, eight injured in Santa Fe Province school shooting
items.description: School shooting in San Cristóbal leaves one dead, eight wounded; 15-year-old attacker entered institution firing shots, killing a 13-year-old and injuring several others. Leer más

items.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
items.description: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

items.title: EU diplomats mark Bucha massacre anniversary, affirms support for Ukraine
items.description: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and foreign ministers of several member states marked the fourth anniversary of the Bucha massacre at a commemoration ceremony in the Kyiv suburb on Tuesday. The ceremony comes amid tensions within the EU over Hungary blocking an aid package for Ukraine.

items.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
items.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief elected vice-president, edging closer to becoming president
items.description: Min Aung Hlaing, Myanmar's junta chief and leader of the 2021 coup, was elected vice-president by the lower house on Tuesday. The country's parliament also elected another vice-president from a military-aligned party and a third will be chosen by the military. A parliamentary vote to select which of the three will be elevated to president is expected later this week.

items.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies
items.description: Nestory Irankunda scores twice as the Socceroos' substitutes spark a 5-1 win over Curaçao in their FIFA Series clash in Melbourne.

items.title: Climate change is making homes shrink and crack
items.description: Periods of intense heat followed by extreme rainfall could cause structural damage to 12 million homes across France, or one in two detached houses, according to the French environment ministry. As homes deteriorate, some owners can lean on insurance for expensive repairs, but many are left with hefty bills. Our France 2 colleagues report.

items.title: As US, Israel and Iran continue fighting, question is how to end 'a war of attrition'
items.description: US President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran's Kharg Island and power grid, were it not to agree to a deal the US sent to Iranian leaders to end the war. This was later followed by fresh strikes from the US and Israel, which Iran responded to by targeting Gulf nations. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah tells us more from Tehran.

items.title: Melbourne childcare centre suspended for 90 days following multiple breaches
items.description: Victoria's new childcare watchdog has suspended operations at Milestones Early Learning Werribee after identifying multiple 'serious and concerning' issues.

items.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
items.description: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers .

items.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
items.description: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

Plus, a chilling trial begins in France into a freemason mafia accused of murder and assault.

items.title: Kenya voter registration drive targets young people ahead of 2027 general election
items.description: In 2024, Kenya’s so-called Gen-Z protests against government corruption and rights abuses swept the country. But since then, many young people have disengaged. Now a new voter registration drive is hoping to get them back on board.

items.title: MinaLima revisit Saint-Exupéry's 'The Little Prince' drawings
items.description: This year, "The Little Prince" turns 80! Over the years, millions of readers have been touched by the classic story written and illustrated by French author Antoine de Saint-Exupéry. To mark this special anniversary, a new edition of the book is being released around the world in 22 languages. It's an illustrated pop-up edition by MinaLima, the studio famous for creating the graphic design of the Harry Potter films. We spoke to the designers Miraphora Mina and Eduardo Lima in Entre Nous.

items.title: Suriname ex-President, Chandrikapersad Santokhi, who probed 1982 political killings, dies at 67
items.description: Santokhi took over a bankrupt Suriname from his predecessor, former dictator Desi Bouterse, and led the country to economic stability, partly supported by an International Monetary Fund program

FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert looks at the takeaways from the press conference, which seemed to be yet another attempt at 'trying to put the best face on a mission' that lacked preparation.

items.description: Europe’s own nature is becoming a risk to itself. Member states have exercised their veto right 48 times since mid-2011. Hungary was the last one to use it on the €90bn financial aid for Ukraine. Ask the Euronews AI chatbot how the EU handles country-blockages.

items.title: Gunmen attack bar in Jos, Nigeria, leaving 33 dead
items.description: Gunmen opened fire on a bar in Jos, the capital of Nigeria's restive Plateau state, sparking retaliation from a mob and killing nearly three dozen people in all, local sources told AFP Monday.

items.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
items.description: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

items.title: Germany: Stranded humpback whale moving again

items.title: Report card issued on Antarctic division's cultural reforms
items.description: Workplace reforms implemented by the Australian Antarctic Division (AAD) after two damning reports into the organisation's culture have led to some improvements but a review has found problems persist.

items.title: Syria's al-Sharaa visits Germany to discuss refugee return, war and recovery
items.description: Ahmed al-Sharaa started a visit to Berlin on March 30 for talks on the Middle East war, rebuilding his country and the return of refugees. The visit is the first to Germany for Syria's interim leader, with critics saying his human rights record is being ignored. It is also the newest attempt by al-Sharaa to kindle a relationship with Western countries, after years of isolation for Syria under dictator Bashar al-Assad. FRANCE 24's Rochelle Ferguson Bouyahi tells us more.

items.title: Dalai Lama backs Pope Leo's peace appeal, urges to end global conflicts
items.description: Calling for global unity, the Dalai Lama urged people worldwide to work towards peace and prayed for an early end to ongoing violence and conflicts

items.title: Industry contamination of soil with toxic metals exposes failure to translate science into policy
items.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Dr. Jagannath Biswakarma, Honorary Senior Research Associate at the University of Bristol. As an environmental scientist, his work focuses on understanding how human activity reshapes the natural systems we depend on, often in ways that remain invisible until their consequences surface in our bodies. He examines the growing global exposure to toxic heavy metals such as cadmium, arsenic, lead, and mercury, substances that, while naturally present in the Earth, are increasingly concentrated through industrial and agricultural processes.

items.title: Finland reports drone crashes, alleges territorial violation
items.description: Two unidentified drones crashed in southern Finland. According to the country's prime minister, the drones may have been Ukrainian drones that strayed into Finnish airspace.

items.title: Malian army denies releasing jihadists to end fuel convoy attacks
items.description: The Malian army denied on Monday that it had released around 200 suspected jihadists in mid-March to secure an end to attacks on fuel convoys that are crippling the landlocked nation's economy.

items.title: Watch: Why a prolonged war with Iran will constrain the U.S. | Data
items.description: When the conflict in West Asia, which began with the U.S. and Israel’s attack on Iran on February 28, escalated into a regional war, analysts said that the war would last as long as Iran had missiles or until the Gulf nations ran out of interceptors. However, with “emergency” military sales, piling monetary costs and a strained supply chain, is the U.S. becoming constrained to keep the war going?

items.title: Argentina take it easy on road to World Cup 2026 title defence
items.description: Since winning the World Cup in 2022, the only notable sides the Albiceleste have faced have been Brazil, Colombia and Uruguay. Otherwise, the run of fixtures has been limited to friendlies against lower-level sides. Leer más

items.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
items.description: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

items.title: Belgium's state-owned railway shines light on Holocaust role
items.description: A Holocaust survivor and the son of a Nazi collaborator have shared their experiences at Brussels' Train World museum. Talking to students, they warned of the dangers of forgetting history.

items.title: News24 | At least 15 US troops wounded in Iran strike on Saudi airbase
items.description: At least 15 American soldiers have been wounded after Iran launched an attack on an airbase in Saudi Arabia, according to news reports, as the conflict started by Israel and the United States enters its second month.

items.title: Egypt business curfew sparks concern among traders and tourism sector
items.description: The government says the move is necessary as fuel costs surge due to disruptions linked to regional conflict, which have driven up import prices and strained national finances.

items.title: Has Pakistan outplayed India in Iran war diplomacy?
items.description: Pakistan is gaining diplomatic visibility during the Iran conflict, while questions are growing over India's quieter strategy.

items.title: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.description: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.title: Spain denies U.S. permission to use its airspace for ‘illegal’ war on Iran
items.description: The country has already barred U.S. from using jointly-operated military bases; Defence Minister calls the war ‘profoundly illegal and unjust’

items.title: Russian students duped into signing contracts with military
items.description: University students are being recruited to serve in drone units fighting Ukraine. They are offered lucrative contracts and promised they won't be sent to the front. Reports suggest those promises are not always kept.

items.title: Man jailed for life for fatal stabbing of 'kind and loving' woman
items.description: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

items.title: A dark cloud that refuses to disperse
items.description: The coup d’état of 1976, and its aftermath, continue to weigh heavily on the collective Argentine mind. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Rubio says US receives ‘positive messages’ from Iran after alleged regime change
items.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Monday voiced hope for working with elements within Iran’s government, saying the United States privately had received positive messages.

items.title: Former Algerian president Liamine Zeroual dies at 84
items.description: Algeria announced three days of national mourning on Sunday after the death of 84-year-old Liamine Zeroual, the former soldier who served as the country's president from 1994 to 1999.

items.title: Iran executes two more convicted of links to banned opposition
items.description: The men were found guilty of attempting “rebellion through involvement in multiple terrorist acts”

items.title: Iran war wipes $120bn off Dubai, Abu Dhabi stock markets
items.description: The UAE's financial markets have been some of the hardest hit by the fallout of the US-Israel war on Iran.

items.title: Indonesia summons Meta and Google over non-compliance with child social media curbs, minister says
items.description: Indonesia requires social media companies ⁠with platforms it deems high risk to deactivate accounts belonging to children under 16

items.title: Kast's Chile axes plans to give papers to almost 200,000 migrants
items.description: President José Antonio Kast's government also announces it will reverse expropriation of Pinochet-era torture centre. Leer más

items.title: 'Cultural heritage belongs to all of humanity': Iranian archaeologist Sepideh Maziar
items.description: A leading Iranian archaeologist has told FRANCE 24 that the destruction of Iranian archeological sites is destroying part of human history. Sepideh Maziar is a senior researcher and lecturer at Goethe University Frankfurt and a founding member of the Society for Iranian Archaeology. Along with her colleagues, she has built an interactive map of sites in Iran that have been damaged. The aim is to try to preserve the memory of the Iranian people and their achievements amid the constant bombing. We spoke to her in Perspective.

items.title: Meta unveils two Ray-Ban smart glasses for prescription wearers starting at $499
Donald Trump willing to end war without reopening Strait of Hormuz, says Wall Street Journal
items.description: In recent days, Mr. Trump and his aides assessed that a mission to pry open the chokepoint would push the conflict beyond his timeline of four to six weeks, the Daily said in a report

items.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
items.description: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for months. When the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

items.title: Inquest into grandmother's death hears of hospital's 'systemic shortcomings'
items.description: A coroner finds the post-surgery care of Robyn Beale at Mater Private Hospital Mackay was not "adequate or appropriate", but the inquest heard the 78-year-old would have died regardless.

items.title: Nepal’s political shift opens a strategic window for India
items.description: The new government in Nepal extends an opportunity to fashion a forward-looking bilateral relationship

items.title: 'Peace is everything': Women-led festival channels message of peace in Eastern DRC
items.description: Congolese rapper channels message of peace at women-led festival in Eastern DRC

items.title: Gulf allies privately tell Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
items.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has told White House officials that a further defanging of Iran's military capabilities and clerical leadership serves the long-term interest of the Gulf region and beyond

items.title: Ukraine's Bucha commemorates four years since massacre
items.description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. When Russian military forces evacuated the city in 2022, hundreds of bodies of men, women, children and elderly people were discovered, with many lying in the street. A delegation of EU ministers is set to arrive to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

items.title: Anti-matter also matters
items.description: Boy From Antimatter is a two-act English-language multi-media musical for both children and adults, based on the book by Cliff Williamson. Leer más

items.title: How to fix Germany's costly health care system
items.description: A special commission has presented a raft of proposals aimed at curbing Germany's spiraling health care costs. But whether the government can impose them is another matter.

items.title: Argentina rating upgrade hinges on reserve build-up, Fitch says
items.description: Argentina’s path to a credit rating upgrade depends on the sustained build-up of foreign-currency reserves, says top exec at Fitch Ratings. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Iran's response to US peace proposal expected Friday, source says
items.description: Iran’s response to a US peace proposal aimed ‌at ending the war in ⁠the Middle East is expected on Friday, according ⁠to a source briefed on the matter.

items.title: Maduro appears in US court as legal team fights narco-terrorism case
items.description: Ousted Venezuelan leader pleads not guilty, declares himself a "prisoner of war," as legal team calls for dismissal of case. Leer más

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
items.description: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

items.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
items.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

items.title: Caputo cheers ‘all-time high’ as INDEC data shows rise in economic activity in January
items.description: Milei and Caputo cheer news that economy grew in line with expectations; Activity in first month of the year up 1.9% year-on–year, by more than 8% from two years ago. Leer más

items.title: Touadera sworn in for third term as Central African Republic president
items.description: Central African President Faustin-Archange Touadera on Monday pledged to buttress security and peace as he was sworn in for a third term in one of the world's poorest and most volatile countries

items.title: News24 | G7 allies press Rubio on US-Iran plans
items.description: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran almost one month into the war in the Middle East, with concern also intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic republic.

items.title: Three Indonesian peacekeepers killed by Israeli attacks in Lebanon
items.description: Two Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon have killed three Indonesian UN peacekeepers in the past 48 hours.

items.title: News24 | UK judge hails Barron Trump for 'lifesaving' actions during assault drama
items.description: A UK judge on Friday hailed the youngest son of US President Donald Trump for his “lifesaving” actions after he remotely witnessed a friend being assaulted.

items.title: Mongolia names new Prime Minister in bid to end legislative deadlock
items.description: Mr. Uchral is a former Minister of Digital Development and Communications, where he promoted transparency reforms and digital governance initiatives. Before entering politics, Mr. Uchral got public attention as a hip-hop artist under the stage name “Timon.”

items.title: Nigeria fuel prices surge 65% amid global oil shock
items.description: Prices have jumped by around 65%, the steepest increase among major African economies. The refinery was expected to reduce imports and stabilize costs, but Nigeria still relies heavily on foreign crude.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Israel's new death penalty law is more of 'an annexation law'
items.description: The European Union, Spain, Germany and other governments have condemned Israel's new law, which would make the death penalty the default sentence for Palestinians who have murdered Israelis and have been tried by the military. 'This law will have an impact on what little is left of Israel's diplomatic standing', says FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky from Jerusalem.

items.title: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
items.description: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

items.title: Expectant mothers in Cuba struggle under fuel blockade
items.description: The BBC's Will Grant speaks to two women experiencing starkly different realities as the country's energy crisis impacts their pregnancies.
